texto: LOVE is measured in GAUGE TWENTY- specifically, love pressure in the surrounding area. If it drops below four percent, though, you may have trouble-the VW might get sad, slow down, or even stop altogether. If this occurs, you have to immediately find/write a story that somehow convinces him that there is more love, caring or compassion in the area than he thinks there is. I can’t tell you how many times this has been a problem for us- how many trips were interrupted because I had to head into the nearest populated town to see if we could find examples of kindness.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Christopher Boucher sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “LOVE is measured in GAUGE TWENTY- specifically, love pressure in the surrounding area. If it drop…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, compassion, love, útil em momentos de estudos e love.

frase_original: LOVE is measured in GAUGE TWENTY- specifically, love pressure in the surrounding area. If it drops below four percent, though, you may have trouble-the VW might get sad, slow down, or even stop altogether. If this occurs, you have to immediately find/write a story that somehow convinces him that there is more love, caring or compassion in the area than he thinks there is. I can’t tell you how many times this has been a problem for us- how many trips were interrupted because I had to head into the nearest populated town to see if we could find examples of kindness.

texto: So go ahead. Do it—open the book. See? You see me, right? And I see you. See? I am reading your face, your eyes, your lips. I know the sufferdust on your brow. I can see you reading and I can tell, too, when you are here, when you’re absent, what you’ve read and how it affects you. There is no more hiding. I see your chords—your fractures, your cold gifts, where and when you’ve hurt people and why. It’s all right there—your stories are written right there on your face!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Christopher Boucher que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “So go ahead. Do it—open the book. See? You see me, right? And I see you. See? I am reading your f…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, reading, right, com foco em reflexao.

I can see you reading and I can tell, too, when you are here, when you’re absent, what you’ve read and how it affects you. There is no more hiding. I see your chords—your fractures, your cold gifts, where and when you’ve hurt people and why. It’s all right there—your stories are written right there on your face!
